tasks.prompt: Write a short essay of 120-180 words on the topic 'Should university students take a part-time job?' Type: opinion essay. State your own clear position in the first paragraph (thesis), then support it with at least two reasons and one concrete example, and end with a short conclusion. Audience: readers of a campus newspaper.
tasks.promptZh: 就“大学生是否应该做兼职工作”写一篇 120-180 词的观点短文，开篇明确立场，用至少两个理由和一个具体例子支持，结尾简短总结。
referencePoints: thesisFocus: take a clear stand on whether students should do part-time work
requiredPoints: at least two distinct reasons plus one concrete example
scoringFocus: logical paragraphing and linking words (firstly, moreover, in conclusion)
scoringFocus: CET-4 level vocabulary and mostly accurate grammar
Reference points guide scoring only; they are not a model essay

tasks.prompt: Write a short essay of 120-180 words on the topic 'Living on campus or renting off campus?' Type: opinion essay. In your first paragraph make clear which option you prefer (thesis), then give two reasons and a brief personal example, and finish with a one-sentence conclusion. Audience: first-year students choosing where to live.
tasks.promptZh: 就“住校还是校外租房”写一篇 120-180 词的观点短文，明确你偏好的选项并用两个理由和一个简短的个人例子支持。
referencePoints: thesisFocus: state a clear preference for campus or off-campus living
requiredPoints: two reasons and one brief personal example
scoringFocus: coherent comparison and clear conclusion
scoringFocus: appropriate connectives and CET-4 grammar
Reference points guide scoring only; they are not a model essay

tasks.prompt: Write a short essay of 120-180 words explaining the phenomenon 'Why are more young people choosing to study in the library rather than in their dorm?' Type: explain-a-phenomenon essay. referencePoints: thesisFocus: identify the trend of preferring the library for study
requiredPoints: two or three plausible causes for the phenomenon

scoringFocus: CET-4 level vocabulary and accurate grammar

Audience: students who feel overwhelmed by online content.
tasks.promptZh: 就“如何应对网络信息过载”写一篇 120-180 词的建议短文，说明信息过载的问题并给出两三条实用建议。
referencePoints: thesisFocus: acknowledge information overload as a real problem
requiredPoints: two or three practical suggestions with reasons
scoringFocus: organized advice and appropriate connectives
scoringFocus: CET-4 vocabulary and mostly accurate grammar
Reference points guide scoring only; they are not a model essay

tasks.prompt: Write a short essay of 120-180 words on the topic 'Should universities offer more practical courses than theoretical ones?' Type: opinion essay. State your position in the opening (thesis), support it with two reasons and one example, then conclude briefly. Audience: a higher-education discussion forum.
tasks.promptZh: 就“大学是否应开设更多实践课而非理论课”写一篇 120-180 词的观点短文，明确立场并用两个理由和一个例子支持。
referencePoints: thesisFocus: take a clear stand on practical versus theoretical courses
requiredPoints: two reasons and one example
scoringFocus: persuasive structure and a clear conclusion
scoringFocus: CET-4 vocabulary and grammar
Reference points guide scoring only; they are not a model essay

tasks.prompt: Write a short essay of 120-180 words explaining the phenomenon 'Why do many young people enjoy night running in cities?' Type: explain-a-phenomenon essay. Describe the trend, give two or three causes, and end with one sentence on what it reflects about urban lifestyles. Audience: a city sports magazine.
tasks.promptZh: 就“为什么很多年轻人喜欢在城市里夜跑”写一篇 120-180 词的现象解释短文，分析原因。
referencePoints: thesisFocus: identify the popularity of night running among young people
requiredPoints: two or three causes for the trend
scoringFocus: clear cause structure and a reflective closing sentence
scoringFocus: CET-4 vocabulary and accurate grammar
Reference points guide scoring only; they are not a model essay

tasks.prompt: Write a short essay of 120-180 words on the topic 'Is it better to study one subject deeply or learn many subjects widely?' Type: opinion essay. Make your view clear in the opening (thesis), give two reasons and one example, and finish with a short conclusion. Audience: students planning their major and electives.
tasks.promptZh: 就“深入钻研一门学科好，还是广泛学习多门学科好”写一篇 120-180 词的观点短文，明确观点并用两个理由和一个例子支持。
referencePoints: thesisFocus: choose between deep study of one subject and broad study of many
requiredPoints: two reasons plus one example
scoringFocus: clear comparison and firm conclusion
scoringFocus: CET-4 grammar and connectives
Reference points guide scoring only; they are not a model essay

tasks.prompt: Write a short essay of 120-180 words describing a simple chart in words on the topic 'Time university students spend on different daily activities.' Type: brief chart description. Imagine a pie chart showing that students spend about 40% of their day on study, 25% on rest and sleep, 20% on entertainment, and 15% on exercise. Describe the data in words (do not draw a chart), point out the most and least time-consuming activities, and add one sentence of brief comment. Audience: a student lifestyle report.
tasks.promptZh: 就“大学生在不同日常活动上花费的时间”用文字描述一个饼图（学习约 40%、休息睡眠 25%、娱乐 20%、运动 15%），指出最多与最少的项目并简短评论。
referencePoints: thesisFocus: open by introducing the chart topic and main data
requiredPoints: describe the four figures in words and name the largest and smallest
scoringFocus: accurate use of comparison and percentage language
scoringFocus: one brief comment plus CET-4 grammar accuracy
Reference points guide scoring only; they are not a model essay

tasks.prompt: Write a short essay of 120-180 words describing a simple chart in words on the topic 'Changes in students' reading habits over five years.' Type: brief chart description. Imagine a line trend in which paper-book reading dropped from 60% to 35% while e-book reading rose from 25% to 55% between 2020 and 2025. Describe the trends in words (do not draw a chart), highlight the opposite directions, and add one sentence of comment. Audience: a campus reading survey report.
tasks.promptZh: 就“五年间学生阅读习惯的变化”用文字描述一个趋势（纸质书阅读从 60% 降到 35%，电子书从 25% 升到 55%），指出相反趋势并简短评论。
referencePoints: thesisFocus: introduce the chart topic and time span
requiredPoints: describe the falling and rising trends in words and contrast them
scoringFocus: accurate trend language (increase, decline, by contrast)
scoringFocus: one brief comment plus CET-4 grammar accuracy
Reference points guide scoring only; they are not a model essay

tasks.prompt: Write a short essay of 120-180 words describing a simple chart in words on the topic 'How students prefer to commute to campus.' Type: brief chart description. Imagine a bar comparison showing that 45% of students walk, 30% ride a bike or e-bike, 15% take the bus, and 10% use other ways. Describe the figures in words (do not draw a chart), point out the most and least common choices, and add one sentence of comment. Audience: a campus transport survey report.
tasks.promptZh: 就“学生上学的通勤方式偏好”用文字描述一个柱状对比（步行 45%、骑车 30%、公交 15%、其他 10%），指出最常见与最少见的方式并简短评论。
referencePoints: thesisFocus: introduce the chart topic and the categories compared
requiredPoints: describe the four figures in words and name the most and least common choices
scoringFocus: accurate comparison and percentage language
scoringFocus: one brief comment plus CET-4 grammar accuracy
Reference points guide scoring only; they are not a model essay

tasks.prompt: Write a short essay of 120-180 words describing a simple chart in words on the topic 'Reasons students give for joining campus activities.' Type: brief chart description. requiredPoints: describe the four figures in words and name the leading and least common reasons
scoringFocus: accurate comparison and percentage language
